spec_match.rationale: The page now preflights the selected BCP 47 language and quality with SpeechRecognition.available(), offers install() for downloadable local packs, and applies processLocally and quality before start().

interactive.rationale: Users can choose a named language, local or browser-selected processing, three quality tiers, install a downloadable pack, and start or stop recognition.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The controls expose the quality-selection workflow and device/language constraints described by the feature.

edge_cases.rationale: Available, downloadable, downloading, unavailable, missing preflight/install APIs, cloud fallback, permission errors, and language-not-supported all produce actionable text.

references.rationale: The page links ChromeStatus feature 5136859632107520 and MDN SpeechRecognition documentation.

conformance.rationale: The feature suite passes 6/6 assertions in this browser for SpeechRecognition, available/install, processLocally, and valid/invalid quality options.

runtime_evidence.rationale: DevTools MCP reproduced the opaque language-not-supported error, then verified that en-US/command reports downloadable with an install action and that browser-selected mode names en-US and reports microphone denial without ambiguity.

telemetry_health.rationale: The repaired controls and fallback paths produced no unexpected console or network errors; expected API/permission outcomes remain visible page state.

design_system.rationale: The new language controls, status panel, and install action use shared variables and preserve readable contrast.

accessibility.rationale: Language and quality controls have native names/states, availability and errors are polite live text, and no error depends on color alone.

summary: The live page hard-coded en-US and called start() without checking whether its local pack existed, so users received only “language-not-supported.” It now names the selected language, preflights language + quality, offers pack installation when downloadable, and explains how to select another language or allow the browser service.
